Name: Fatso's Last Stand (Ukrainian Village)
Description: My go-to stop in the city when I want something casual that still feels very Chicago. It’s the kind of place I’d bring someone to if they wanted a real neighborhood vibe.

Name: Johnny's IceHouse West
Description: This place is pure Chicago sports energy and it always feels busy in the best way. If someone’s visiting and wants to feel the hockey culture here, this is an easy pick.

Name: Saddle & Cycle Club
Description: This spot feels classic Chicago and it’s tied to a very specific part of the city. It’s one of those places that makes you think about how old and layered Chicago really is.

Name: Beverly Country Club
Description: Beverly has that old-school golf feel and it’s a big part of the South Side identity. It’s a place I associate with tradition and a quieter side of the city.

Name: UChicago Lab Schools (Blaine Hall)
Description: This is a location that stands out to me because it’s connected to a serious academic environment and a unique part of Chicago. It feels like a landmark for learning, not just a building.

Name: Millennium Park
Description: This is the classic first stop because it instantly looks like Chicago in photos. Even if you’ve seen it online a million times, it hits different in person.

Name: Art Institute of Chicago
Description: This is one of those places where you can spend way longer than you planned. It’s also a good ‘Chicago is a world city’ proof point when someone visits.

Name: Navy Pier
Description: Navy Pier is touristy, but it’s also a guaranteed win if you want views and people-watching. It’s an easy way to get someone excited about the lakefront.

Name: Garfield Park Conservatory
Address: 300 N Central Park Ave, Chicago, IL 60624
Description: This feels like a hidden gem because it’s such a strong change of scenery from the streets. It’s a great spot when you want something calm but still impressive.

Name: Wrigley Field
Description: Even if you don’t care about baseball, the area around Wrigley is a whole experience. It’s one of the easiest places to feel the city’s sports culture.
